issues.comment: this.get('host.category.id') accesses category.id without checking if host.category is null/undefined, which will cause TypeError when category is null.
obj[k].map() is called without checking if obj[k] is null/undefined first, which will cause TypeError when the _ids field contains null.
EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first can return nil, but line 11 calls save_host(host) without nil check, causing NoMethodError when host is nil.
EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first can return nil, but line 16 calls host.destroy without nil check, causing NoMethodError when host is nil.
params[:embeddable_host][:host] accesses nested hash without checking if params[:embeddable_host] exists, causing NoMethodError when parameter structure is invalid.
EmbeddableHost.host_allowed?(request.referer) is called without checking if request.referer is nil first, which could cause issues in host validation logic.
self.host.sub!() is called without checking if self.host is nil, causing NoMethodError when host attribute is nil during validation.
execute() result is accessed with [0]['id'] without checking if query returned any rows, causing IndexError when no matching embed_category exists.
execute() result is accessed with [0]['value'] without checking if query returned any rows, causing IndexError when uncategorized_category_id setting doesn't exist.
String interpolation '#{h}' in SQL execute statement creates SQL injection vulnerability, as host values are inserted directly without escaping or parameterization.
File contains category fabricator definitions but filename suggests it should contain embeddable_host fabricators, indicating the file contents are completely wrong.
File contains category fabricator definitions instead of embeddable_host fabricators as indicated by filename, meaning the fabricator files have been swapped.
